note.text: Longstanding allergic asthma with multiple aeroallergen sensitivities. Poorly controlled despite high-dose ICS/LABA; 3 ED visits and 1 hospitalization in past 12 months prior to this visit.

coding.display: budesonide 0.16 MG/ACTUAT / formoterol fumarate 0.0045 MG/ACTUAT Metered Dose Inhaler [Symbicort 160/4.5]

dosageInstruction.text: Inhale 2 puffs by mouth twice daily

dosageInstruction.text: Inhale 2 puffs by mouth every 4-6 hours as needed

resource.conclusion: Moderate airflow obstruction (FEV1 70% predicted, FEV1/FVC 70%) despite high-dose ICS/LABA. Poor symptom control (ACT 10) with recurrent exacerbations.
Lungs clear, no acute infiltrate, effusion, or hyperinflation. Normal cardiomediastinal silhouette. No structural explanation for symptoms; findings consistent with asthma.

code.text: CBC with Allergic/Type 2 Inflammation Workup (Eosinophils, Total IgE, Allergen-specific IgE)

resource.conclusion: Markedly elevated total IgE (480 IU/mL) and blood eosinophils (600/uL) with positive dust mite-specific IgE, consistent with Type 2-high severe allergic asthma. Patient meets criteria for anti-IgE biologic therapy (omalizumab) given IgE 30-1500 IU/mL range and body weight.

resource.title: Severe Asthma Consult - Biologic Candidacy Evaluation

text.div: Referred for evaluation of poorly controlled asthma despite high-dose ICS/LABA. Reports daily symptoms, frequent nighttime awakening, and 3 ED visits plus 1 hospitalization in the past year. Known dust mite and cat dander sensitivities.

VS: BP 114/72, HR 78, RR 18, SpO2 95%. FEV1 2.1L (70% predicted), FEV1/FVC 70%, PEF 290 L/min. ACT score 10 (very poorly controlled). Labs: Total IgE 480 IU/mL, eosinophils 600/uL, dust mite-specific IgE class 3 positive. CXR clear.
Severe persistent allergic (Type 2-high) asthma, uncontrolled on maximal ICS/LABA therapy. Meets criteria for anti-IgE biologic add-on therapy.

Continue Symbicort 160/4.5 and albuterol PRN. Discussed omalizumab (Xolair) candidacy pending insurance authorization; will initiate at next visit or sooner if exacerbation occurs. Allergen avoidance counseling provided.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 5 tablets (50 mg) by mouth once daily for 5 days

coding.display: C reactive protein [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

resource.title: Emergency Department Visit - Severe Asthma Exacerbation

text.div: Acute dyspnea and wheeze following exposure to a cat at a friend's home. Rapid onset over 1 hour with minimal relief from rescue inhaler.
VS: BP 128/80, HR 116, RR 27, SpO2 90% RA, Temp 37.0C. Diffuse wheeze, accessory muscle use. CRP 6.5 mg/L.
Severe acute asthma exacerbation, allergen-triggered (cat exposure), in setting of known severe persistent allergic asthma.

Nebulized bronchodilators, IV corticosteroids in ED, transitioned to oral prednisone 50mg daily x5 days. Improved to SpO2 96% RA, discharged. Urgent pulmonology follow-up in 3 days to finalize omalizumab initiation given recurrent severe exacerbations.

dosageInstruction.text: 225 mg (1.5 mL) subcutaneously every 2 weeks, dosed per baseline IgE and body weight per prescribing table

note.text: Initiated after 2nd severe exacerbation in 6 months despite maximal ICS/LABA; baseline IgE 480 IU/mL, weight 68kg qualifies per FDA dosing table.

resource.conclusion: Further decline in FEV1 (1.8L) and ACT (8) since second severe exacerbation. Confirms need for biologic add-on therapy; omalizumab initiated this visit.

text.div: Follow-up 3 days after 2nd severe exacerbation in 6 months (cat allergen triggered). Continues daily symptoms despite maximal ICS/LABA and good adherence.
VS: BP 116/74, HR 82, RR 19, SpO2 94%, Weight 68kg. FEV1 1.8L, FEV1/FVC 62%, PEF 230 L/min. ACT score 8 (very poorly controlled).
Severe persistent allergic asthma, uncontrolled despite maximal inhaled therapy and good adherence. Confirmed candidate for anti-IgE biologic.
Initiate omalizumab 225mg subcutaneously every 2 weeks. Continue Symbicort 160/4.5 and albuterol PRN. Reassess response at 3 months per GINA biologic response criteria.

resource.conclusion: Marked improvement since starting omalizumab 3 months ago: FEV1 1.8L->2.4L, FEV1/FVC normalized to 75%. No exacerbations since biologic initiation.

resource.title: Severe Asthma Follow-up - 3 Months on Omalizumab

text.div: Reports substantial improvement since starting omalizumab injections. No ED visits or oral steroid courses since initiation. Rescue inhaler use down to about once weekly.
VS: BP 112/70, HR 76, RR 16, SpO2 97%. FEV1 2.4L (up from 1.8L), FEV1/FVC 75% (normalized), PEF 370 L/min. ACT score 18, improving.
Severe allergic asthma responding well to omalizumab; zero exacerbations in 3 months since biologic initiation, marked spirometric improvement.

Continue omalizumab 225mg every 2 weeks, Symbicort 160/4.5, and albuterol PRN. Continue allergen avoidance. Reassess ACT and spirometry at next visit; consider ICS de-escalation if continued good control.

coding.display: Eosinophils [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count

resource.conclusion: Spirometry now within normal limits (FEV1 2.7L, FEV1/FVC 79%). ACT 22 (well controlled). Eosinophils normalized to 280/uL. Excellent response to omalizumab; no exacerbations in 5 months.

resource.title: Severe Asthma Follow-up - 5 Months on Omalizumab

text.div: Feeling well, no rescue inhaler use in the past month, no exacerbations since starting omalizumab 5 months ago. Tolerating injections well with no adverse effects.
VS: BP 110/70, HR 74, RR 16, SpO2 98%. FEV1 2.7L, FEV1/FVC 79% (normalized), PEF 410 L/min. ACT score 22 (well controlled). Eosinophils normalized to 280/uL.
Severe allergic asthma, now well controlled on omalizumab plus high-dose ICS/LABA. Spirometry normalized, biomarkers improved, zero exacerbations in 5 months.
Continue omalizumab 225mg every 2 weeks and Symbicort 160/4.5. Discuss potential ICS step-down at next visit given sustained control. Continue allergen avoidance measures. Follow-up in 3 months.
